steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is "collection" (singular). "of oil paintings" is a prepositional phrase modifying "collection," and "which includes James Ensor's Tribulations of Saint Anthony and Yasuo Kuniyoshi's Self-Portrait as a Golf Player" is a nonrestrictive relative clause. Neither changes the number of the subject. The verb must agree with the singular "collection."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "remain": plural present-tense verb; requires a plural subject like "they." Disagrees with singular "collection." ✗
  • "have remained": plural present-perfect verb; same problem. ✗
  • "remains": singular present-tense verb. "The collection remains" — subject and verb agree. ✓
  • "have been remaining": plural progressive perfect; disagrees with "collection" and is stylistically awkward regardless. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
